turns.title: Batting Analyst
turns.recommendation: Treat this as a two-over launch window: protect MS Dhoni, target the shorter side, and keep 44 needed visible without forcing low-percentage singles.
risks: Two-paced surface can turn hard length into miscues
A wicket before the 18th over shrinks finishing options
counters: Counter to prior: baseline field pressure. Batting should dictate matchups before the defense locks yorker fields.

turns.title: Bowling Analyst
turns.recommendation: Hold Jasprit Bumrah for overs 18 and 20, use pace-off into the pitch now, and deny the on-pace slot ball while dew is making grip harder.
risks: Dew can turn slower balls into hittable floaters
Predictable wide yorkers invite premeditated movement
counters: Counter to prior: Treat this as a two-over launch window: protect MS Dhoni, target the shorter side, and keep 44 needed visible without forcing low-percentage singles.. Bowling value comes from forcing the hitter to create pace.

turns.title: Fielding Analyst
turns.recommendation: Set long-on, deep midwicket, deep square, third, and fine leg back; keep one catching cover for the slower-ball miscue.
risks: Singles through cover can release pressure
Fine margins on boundary riders at Pune
counters: Counter to prior: Hold Jasprit Bumrah for overs 18 and 20, use pace-off into the pitch now, and deny the on-pace slot ball while dew is making grip harder.. Field shape must sell the bowler plan, not react after contact.

turns.title: Impact Player Analyst
turns.recommendation: Keep the Impact Player trigger live for the next wicket or the 18th over; use it only if it improves boundary hitting or death bowling by a clear matchup.
risks: Late substitution can disrupt settled roles
Using the option too early may strand a better death-over matchup
counters: Counter to prior: Set long-on, deep midwicket, deep square, third, and fine leg back; keep one catching cover for the slower-ball miscue.. The substitution should be a leverage tool, not a panic button.

decision.call: Freeze the next six balls around MS Dhoni: chase boundary matchups, but do not spend the Impact Player unless the next wicket falls or a death-over specialist becomes decisive.
decision.battingPlan: One batter attacks the matchup while the other guarantees strike reset. Avoid cross-bat swings until the bowler misses length.
decision.bowlingPlan: Defend with pace-off hard length now, then reserve the strongest death option for overs 18 and 20.
decision.fieldPlan: Protect leg-side boundaries with one catching off-side position to keep wicket pressure alive.
decision.impactPlayerPlan: Delay activation until the role is specific: finisher if chasing loses a wicket, death bowler if defending the final two overs.
riskControls: Two-paced surface can turn hard length into miscues
A wicket before the 18th over shrinks finishing options
Dew can turn slower balls into hittable floaters
Predictable wide yorkers invite premeditated movement
decision.winProbabilitySwing: Estimated plus 6-9% if execution keeps wickets intact through the next over.
